title: Ensure that the kubeAPIQPS [--event-qps] argument is set to 0 or a level which ensures appropriate event capture

input_row.title: Ensure that the kubeAPIQPS [--event-qps] argument is set to 0 or a level which ensures appropriate event capture
input_row.description: Security relevant information should be captured. The --event-qps flag on the Kubelet can be used to limit the rate at which events are gathered. Setting this too low could result in relevant events not being logged, however the unlimited setting of 0 could result in a denial of service on the kubelet.
input_row.rationale: It is important to capture all events and not restrict event creation. Events are an important source of security information and analytics that ensure that your environment is consistently monitored using the event data.
input_row.audit: OpenShift uses the kubeAPIQPS argument and sets it to a default value of 50 . When this value is set to > 0, event creations per second are limited to the value set. If this value is set to 0 , event creations per second are unlimited. Run the following command on each machine pool. For example: for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'); do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host more /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf; done oc get machineconfig 01-worker-kubelet -o yaml | grep --color kubeAPIQPS%3A%2050 oc get machineconfig 01-master-kubelet -o yaml | grep --color kubeAPIQPS%3A%2050 229 | P a g e Review the value set for the kubeAPIQPS argument and determine whether this has been set to an appropriate level for the cluster. If this value is set to 0 , event creations per second are unlimited.
input_row.remediation: Follow the documentation to edit kubelet parameters https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.5/scalability_and_performance/recommended-host-practices.html#create-a-kubeletconfig-crd-to-edit-kubelet-parameters KubeAPIQPS: <QPS>
input_row.impact: Setting this parameter to 0 could result in a denial of service condition due to excessive events being created. The cluster's event processing and storage systems should be scaled to handle expected event loads.
input_row.default_value: By default, the kubeAPIQPS argument is set to 50 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control regarding `kubeAPIQPS` can be verified by querying relevant settings through the IBM Cloud APIs/IBM Cloud CLI. This parameter is part of the Kubernetes configuration and can be accessed programmatically.
- The audit field contains clear instructions on how to check the kubeAPIQPS value.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_kubeAPIQPS_event_rate_limit

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated audit program would check the following:

- **IBM Cloud CLI Commands:**
  - Use a combination of commands to fetch kubelet configuration details from the OpenShift cluster.
  
- **Proposed Commands:**
  - For each node: 
    ```bash
    for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'); do 
       oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host more /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf; 
    done
    ```
  - Check machine configurations:
    ```bash
    oc get machineconfig 01-worker-kubelet -o yaml | grep kubeAPIQPS
    oc get machineconfig 01-master-kubelet -o yaml | grep kubeAPIQPS
    ```

- **Configuration Properties to Validate:**
  - Verify that the `kubeAPIQPS` flag is set to 0 or a value that suits your event capturing needs (e.g., 50 as per default).

- **Expected Values:**
  - `kubeAPIQPS` should be either set to 0 (unlimited event capture) or a positive integer that meets the operational requirements without causing a denial of service.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** If `kubeAPIQPS` is configured correctly as per the above requirements.
  - **Fail:** If `kubeAPIQPS` is set incorrectly (like a very low number that can restrict events), or if it's missing entirely from the configuration.

This configuration will ensure that the auditing mechanism is automated and leverages available API and CLI capabilities in IBM Cloud, confirming compliance with the CIS benchmark for OpenShift compliance checks.
